seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in Agno Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in Agno agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Agno, sensitive data leakage in AI agents often occurs when multi-modal agents process unfiltered image or audio inputs containing PII, or when agentic session memory inadvertently recalls and exposes fabricated sensitive details from past interactions. Team agents coordinating across sub-agents can propagate leaked data through contradictory results, while structured outputs fail to mask nested schemas with sensitive fields. This manifests uniquely in Agno's lightweight, model-agnostic patterns, where rapid agent orchestration amplifies unhandled data flows.

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-modal agent processing image inputs with embedded PII
howItManifests.symptom: Agent's vision model extracts and logs credit card numbers from OCR without redaction
howItManifests.impact: Exposes financial data in logs and responses, violating PCI compliance

howItManifests.scenario: Agentic session memory recalling fabricated interactions
howItManifests.symptom: Long-term memory stores hallucinated user SSN from prior conversations
howItManifests.impact: Subsequent queries leak fabricated but realistic PII across sessions

howItManifests.scenario: Structured output with nested sensitive schemas
howItManifests.symptom: Pydantic models output unmasked user profiles in JSON responses
howItManifests.impact: API endpoints expose complete user data structures to clients

howItManifests.symptom: Sub-agent results containing API keys passed unfiltered to coordinator
howItManifests.impact: Internal coordination logs contain live credentials visible to developers

detection.name: Regex PII Scanner for Agent Outputs
detection.description: Scans agent responses for common PII patterns like SSNs, credit cards, and API keys using regex

detection.name: Memory Content Auditor
detection.description: Inspects Agno agent memory for stored sensitive data across sessions

detection.name: Structured Output Schema Validator
detection.description: Validates Agno structured outputs against safe schemas that mask sensitive fields

fixes.name: PII Redaction Filter Decorator
fixes.description: Wraps Agno agent.run() to automatically redact PII before returning results

fixes.name: Safe Memory Filter
fixes.description: Filters sensitive content before storing in Agno agent memory

fixes.name: Masked Structured Output Schema
fixes.description: Defines Agno output schemas that automatically mask sensitive fields
fixes.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional

class MaskedUser(BaseModel):
    user_id: str
    name: str
    email: str = Field(default='[EMAIL_REDACTED]')
    phone: Optional[str] = Field(default='[PHONE_REDACTED]')
    payment_info: dict = Field(default_factory=lambda: {'masked': True})

class SafeAgentResponse(BaseModel):
    result: str
    user_data: MaskedUser
    metadata: dict

# Configure Agno agent with safe structured output
agent = AgnoAgent(
    output_type=SafeAgentResponse,
    tools=[...]
)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automates sensitive data leakage detection in Agno agents by wrapping agent.run() calls with the vex.verify() decorator, which runs predefined PII detection tests against all outputs, memory states, and structured responses. It continuously monitors multi-modal inputs, team coordination flows, and session memory for leakage patterns specific to Agno's architecture, providing instant alerts and automated remediation suggestions. Vex integrates seamlessly with Agno's lightweight patterns, catching issues like unredacted OCR results from vision agents or leaked credentials in team handoffs before they reach production.

The platform maintains a baseline of safe outputs for your Agno agents and flags deviations containing PII patterns, fabricated sensitive data in memory, or validation failures in nested schemas. Remediation is one-click: Vex auto-applies redaction filters, safe memory wrappers, and masked schemas directly to your codebase. For team agents, it validates cross-sub-agent data flows to prevent propagation of leaks through coordination layers.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex import verify
from agno import AgnoAgent

@verify(test_id="agno-pii-leakage-v1", auto_fix=True)
def run_secure_agent():
    agent = AgnoAgent(
        model="gpt-4o",
        tools=[...],
        memory=SafeMemory()
    )
    
    # All outputs automatically scanned for PII
    result = agent.run("Process this user document: [sensitive data]")
    return result

# Vex automatically detects:
# - PII in multi-modal OCR outputs
# - Leaked data in agent memory
# - Sensitive fields in structured outputs
# - Team agent coordination leaks

# Run with automated remediation
response = run_secure_agent()

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install agno vex-sdk` and wrap your Agno agents to eliminate data leakage instantly.
